name: Comparing Socio-technical Design Principles with Guidelines for Human-centered AI
summary: arXiv:2607.10331v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human-centered AI (HCAI) refers to guidelines or principles that aim on ethi-cally oriented design of systems. We compare HCAI- guidelines with princ...
description: arXiv:2607.10331v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human-centered AI (HCAI) refers to guidelines or principles that aim on ethi-cally oriented design of systems. We compare HCAI- guidelines with princi-ples of socio-technical systems that emerged in the context of conventional in-formation technology. The comparison leads to a revision of socio-technical heuristics by including aspects of AI-usage...
author: Thomas Herrmann
